task_completion.description: The catenary function f(x) is entered into GeoGebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra View in GeoGebra shows the function f(x) = 630 - 128*(cosh(x/128) - 1) or a mathematically equivalent expression.

task_completion.description: The parabolic function g(x) is entered into GeoGebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra View in GeoGebra shows the function g(x) = 630*(1 - (x/315)^2) or a mathematically equivalent expression (like y = -0.00635x² + 630).

task_completion.description: Both curves are visible in the Graphics View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphics View is zoomed and panned appropriately so that both arch-shaped curves are visible. The axes should show a scale large enough to display the peak at y=630 and the base at x=±315.

task_completion.description: A text annotation labeling or comparing the curves is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box is visible within the GeoGebra Graphics View that contains descriptive text labeling the curves (e.g., 'Catenary vs Parabola') or comparing them.

task_completion.description: The project is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra window title bar displays 'gateway_arch_comparison.ggb', or a file manager/save dialog shows the file saved as 'gateway_arch_comparison.ggb' in the ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to create the models.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual GeoGebra application interface being interacted with, rather than a text editor or terminal script generating the .ggb file directly.

integrity.description: The curves are genuinely rendered by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The curves appear as mathematical plots in the Graphics View corresponding to the equations in the Algebra View, not as pasted images or mockups.

integrity.description: The text annotation is a native GeoGebra text object.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text annotation appears as a standard GeoGebra text object in the Graphics View (and likely listed in the Algebra View), rather than edited into the image post-hoc.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'gateway_arch_comparison.ggb'. The catenary function must include 'cosh' (hyperbolic cosine). The parabola function must include an x^2 or x² term. Both mathematical models represent the Gateway Arch with a height of 630 and a half-width of 315 (total width 630). To verify that both curves are visible, look for the Graphics View to be zoomed out sufficiently to show y-values up to at least 630 and x-values spanning from -315 to 315.
